LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS NADER SHABAHANGI, PACIFIC INSTITUTE, AND AGESONG, INC.'S SPECIAL DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM BECAUSE THE COMPLAINT FAILS TO ALLEGE THAT ALL REQUIRED CONDITIONS WERE SATISFIED. PLAINTIFF ALSO HAS LEAVE TO AMEND IN ORDER TO INCLUDE THE CONCEDEDLY MISSING PAGES OF THE AGREEMENT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY CAUSE OF ACTION. NEXT, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AND MISREPRESENTATION AS AGAINST DEFENDANT SHABAHANGI. HOWEVER, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THESE CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANT PACIFIC INSTITUTE AS THE COMPLAINT IS UNCLEAR AS TO WHETHER ANYONE WITH AUTHORITY TO ACT FOR PACIFIC INSTITUTE MADE MISREPRESENTATIONS. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONSPIRACY BECAUSE THERE IS A VIABLE CLAIM FOR FRAUD AS THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY THE REQUIRED ELEMENTS. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE CLAIMS FOR CONSTRUCTIVE AND RESULTING TRUSTS. FINALLY, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE AS THE COMPLAINT MEETS THE PLEADING REQUIREMENTS FOR THESE CLAIMS. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS NADER SHABAHANGI, PACIFIC INSTITUTE AND AGESONG, INC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART, DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FORECLOSURE AND AS TO THE NINTH CAUSE FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT OPPOSE THE DEMURRER TO THE EIGHTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION ON THE BASIS OF THE STANDSTILL PROVISION. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF WRITTEN CONTRACT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOT A SHAM PLEADING. PLAINTIFF PLEADS SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE A MATERIAL BREACH OF CONTRACT AND CONTRACTUAL DAMAGES. (COMPLAINT 13(B-D AND F-H) AND 18-20.) DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY IS DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS ARE CLEARLY MADE AGAINST DEFENDANT NADER AND NOT SYNERGY LLC. (COMPLAINT 22-24.) DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION IS DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLED IN THE COMPLAINT THE CAPACITIES IN WHICH NADER WAS ACTING WHEN HE MADE THE ALLEGED NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATIONS AND ENGAGED IN THE ALLEGED NEGLIGENT INTERFERENCE WITH PROTECTIVE ECONOMIC RELATIONS. (COMPLAINT 12 AND 13.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS NADER SHABAHANGI, PACIFIC INSTITUTE AND AGESONG, INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART, DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A PERMANENT INJUNCTION IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ESTABLISH WHY MONEY DAMAGES ARE AN INADEQUATE REMEDY. IF PLAINTIFF PREVAILS IN THIS LAWSUIT, THE FINAL JUDGMENT WILL BE CONCLUSIVE AND ENFORCEABLE BY PLAINTIFF, RENDERING THE NEED FOR AN INJUNCTION UNNECESSARY. (CCP 577, 1908 SUBD. (A)(2).) DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR FORECLOSURE OF DEEDS OF TRUST AND THE APPOINTMENT OF A RECEIVER IS DENIED AS MOOT AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FORECLOSURE AND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/AJR

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS NADER SHABAHANGI AND PACIFIC INSTITUTE, AGESONG, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE REQUEST FOR A PERMANENT INJUNCTION IS GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS IT FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE WHY THERE IS AN INADEQUATE REMEDY AT LAW. THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS DENIED AS MOOT AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AGAINST DEFENDANT PACIFIC INSTITUTE IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AS AGAINST DEFENDANT SHABAHANGI AS THERE IS A VIABLE CLAIM FOR FRAUD THAT SUPPORTS A PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONSPIRACY AGAINST BOTH DEFENDANTS. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR RESULTING AND CONSTRUCTIVE TRUSTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-26-2017 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-25-2016 Continued To Jan-30-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-04-2015 Set For Trial On Aug-22-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-28-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JENNIFER L. STARK OF ROSEN BIEN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-433-6830 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS NADER SHABAHANGI, PACIFIC INSTITUTE and AGESONG, INC's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained in part, denied in part. Defendants' demurrer is sustained as to the eighth cause of action for foreclosure and as to the ninth cause for specific performance without leave to amend. Plaintiff does not oppose the demurrer to the eighth and ninth causes of action on the basis of the standstill provision. Defendants' demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of written contract is denied. Plaintiff's second amended complaint is not a sham pleading. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to allege a material breach of contract and contractual damages. (Complaint 13(b-d and f-h) and 18-20.) Defendants' demurrer to Plaintiff's second cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is denied because Plaintiff's allegations are clearly made against Defendant Nader and not Synergy LLC. (Complaint 22-24.) Defendants' demurrer to the fifth and seventh causes of action is denied because Plaintiff has sufficiently pled in the complaint the capacities in which Nader was acting when he made the alleged negligent misrepresentations and engaged in the alleged negligent interference with protective economic relations. (Complaint 12 and 13.) Order signed in open court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-28-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JENNIFER L. STARK OF ROSEN BIEN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-433-6830 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. EFENDANTS NADER SHABAHANGI, PACIFIC INSTITUTE AND AGESONG, INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted in part, denied in part. Defendants' motion to strike Plaintiff's request for a permanent injunction is granted without leave to amend. Plaintiff has failed to establish why money damages are an inadequate remedy. If Plaintiff prevails in this lawsuit, the final judgment will be conclusive and enforceable by Plaintiff, rendering the need for an injunction unnecessary. (CCP 577, 1908 subd. (a)(2).) Defendants' motion to strike Plaintiff's request for foreclosure of deeds of trust and the appointment of a receiver is denied as moot as to the cause of action for foreclosure and as to the cause of action for specific performance in light of the ruling on Defendants' demurrer. Order signed in open court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ajr

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS NADER SHABAHANGI and PACIFIC INSTITUTE, AGESONG, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
The Special Demurrer is overruled. The parties in this action are not the same parties as those in Shabahangi v. Shabahangi (SF Sup. Ct. Case NO. CGC-11-508919), which is fatal to Defendants' request under CCP 430.10(c), and Defendants' apparent attempt in reply to abandon the request pursuant to CCP 430.10(c) and instead seek a stay under CRC 3.515 is also denied. No party has petitioned for coordination of this or the earlier matter. Next, none of the causes of action are barred by the statute of limitations because the Complaint sufficiently alleges that Plaintiff did not have a reason to discover the breach until Plaintiff received financial documents in connection with another lawsuit. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the breach of contract claim because the Complaint fails to allege that all required conditions were satisfied. Plaintiff's allegation that "all conditions precedent to the payments . . . had been met" is insufficient due to the qualifying language. Plaintiff also has leave to amend in order to include the concededly missing pages of the agreement. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the breach of fiduciary duty cause of action. First, the Complaint sufficiently alleges the existence of a fiduciary relationship. Second, the Complaint sufficiently alleges conduct that, if true, amounts to a breach of fiduciary duty. However, the inconsistent allegations as to Defendant Shabahangi's status as a board member and/or his authority to control Pacific Institute renders the Complaint uncertain and confusing, and does not sufficiently alert the Defendants as to the charges asserted against them. Next, the Demurrer is overruled as to the causes of action for fraud and misrepresentation as against Defendant Shabahangi. However, the Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to these causes of action against Defendant Pacific Institute as the Complaint is unclear as to whether anyone with authority to act for Pacific Institute made misrepresentations. The Demurrer is overruled as to the cause of action for conspiracy because there is a viable claim for fraud as the Complaint sufficiently the required elements. The Demurrer is overruled as to the claims for constructive and resulting trusts. While constructive and resulting trusts are in fact remedies, they may be pled in the Complaint to allege a right to seek such remedies. Finally, the Demurrer is overruled as to the causes of action for interference with prospective economic advantage as the Complaint meets the pleading requirements for these claims. (See Korea Supply Co. v. Lockheed Martin Corp. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1134, 1153 [plaintiff is not required to plead that defendant acted with the specific intent, or purpose, of disrupting plaintiff's prospective economic advantage; instead, it is sufficient to plead that defendant knew that the interference was certain or substantially certain to occur.]. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

Defendants Nader Shabahangi, Pacific Institute, Agesong, Inc.'S Demurrer To Complaint (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Nov-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Defendants Nader Shabahangi, Pacific Institute, Agesong, Inc.'S Demurrer To Complaint is a duplicate entry.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS NADER SHABAHANGI and PACIFIC INSTITUTE, AGESONG, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is granted in part and denied in part. The Motion to Strike the request for a permanent injunction is granted with 10 days leave to amend as it fails to demonstrate why there is an inadequate remedy at law. The Motion to Strike the request for punitive damages is denied as moot as to the cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty and as to the cause of action for fraud against Defendant Pacific Institute in light of the ruling on Defendants' Demurrer. The Motion is denied as to the cause of action for fraud as against Defendant Shabahangi as there is a viable claim for fraud that supports a prayer for punitive damages. The Motion is denied as to the cause of action for conspiracy against both Defendants. The Motion is granted as to the causes of action for resulting and constructive trusts. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (see mini-minutes for complete ruling) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)
